world.name: Digital Asset Fragmentation Simulation
world.domain: Digital asset governance, custody, and market-structure divergence
world.geography: US / EU / offshore crypto and tokenized finance environment
world.boundedDescription: A bounded Narrative World covering stablecoin policy, exchange supervision, custody doctrine, tokenized collateral, and market-structure divergence across regulated and offshore digital asset venues.
world.summary: The world starts from mixed but interoperable digital asset governance assumptions. Over 18 months, custody failures, legal classification disputes, stablecoin rules, and venue divergence split the market into structurally fragmented governance zones.

events.label: T0
events.title: Baseline Interoperable Digital Asset Regime
events.description: Baseline environment with mixed regulation but still-linked custody, exchange, and tokenized finance assumptions.

events.label: D1
events.title: Custody Solvency Rumor
events.description: Rumors around a major custody provider raise immediate questions about segregation, reserves, and recoverability.

events.label: D2
events.title: Stablecoin Reserve Review Framework
events.description: A reserve review framework formalizes stricter reserve and redemption expectations for systemically important stablecoins.

events.label: D3
events.title: Exchange Routing Transparency Leak
events.description: A routing leak exposes fragmented liquidity treatment across affiliated venues and market makers.

events.label: D4
events.title: Token Classification Dispute Precedent
events.description: A fresh legal precedent widens disagreement over security, commodity, and payment-token classification.

events.label: D5
events.title: Qualified Custody Rule Expansion
events.description: Qualified custody expansion changes assumptions about who can hold tokenized assets on behalf of institutions.

events.label: D6
events.title: Offshore Venue Capital Migration
events.description: Capital migrates toward offshore venues with divergent listing and leverage standards.

events.label: D7
events.title: Bridge Security Failure
events.description: A cross-chain bridge failure weakens assumptions about interoperable settlement reliability.

events.label: D8
events.title: Tokenized Collateral Supervisory Act
events.description: A supervisory act formalizes separate treatment for tokenized collateral and exchange-held assets.

events.label: D9
events.title: Stablecoin Access Restriction Leak
events.description: A leaked restriction proposal indicates future segmentation of stablecoin access by jurisdiction and institution type.

events.label: D10
events.title: Exchange Insurance Repricing
events.description: Insurance repricing embeds assumptions of persistent venue differentiation and operational risk.

events.label: D11
events.title: Settlement Network Outage Shock
events.description: A settlement-network outage reinforces the narrative of fragmented and non-fungible digital asset infrastructure.

events.label: D12
events.title: Reciprocal Listing Restriction Package
events.description: Listing restrictions widen structural separation between regulated and offshore token universes.

events.label: D13
events.title: Custody Collateral Haircut Revision
events.description: Collateral haircuts diverge sharply across jurisdictions, reinforcing durable fragmentation in market access.

events.label: D14
events.title: Dual-Zone Digital Asset Doctrine
events.description: A dual-zone doctrine formalizes separate governance zones for compliant and offshore digital asset ecosystems.
